item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. I feel like you've used just the right number of iterations to reinforce the important motifs without dragging on, and just the right amount of weirdness that the reader can still piece together some type of narrative, without it being either muddied or watered down by, as they say, "lol random" elements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) I don't get it
2) It reminds me of a number of other SCPs I can't bring to mind at the moment, not least of which is A Light That Died
3) I kinda really wish I'd gotten to this number first

Despite that, I thoroughly enjoyed not understanding an iota of this article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's dope.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i feel like this fails the Conservation of WTF principle and just ends up a big muddled mess of words i can't read. i want to feel like the .aic is trying to give this story direction, but there's just so much random going on, it's hard to even keep track of what he's saying. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
teeth: yes

I don't know what i just read, awesome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well i can't because i'm dummy. care to explain ?

item.title: +1 more
item.content:encoded: I would like to say that your spelling of "readimg" in this comment should not be "fixed" because it is perfect as is and feels perfectly in place for what this article is.
Thank you for readimg, everyone, and hold onto your teeth!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TEETH: +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm happy this wasn't just a collection of nonsense phrases, and that there actually was a deeper story here. Despite the, ahem, surrealism of the entire article, I felt compelled to read it deeply and was very well-rewarded for figuring out what was going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those few SCP articles that make me produce a confused look on my face. Weird.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Putting the Surrealist in Surrealistics. The previous Surrealistics articles have managed to be weird, but not properly surrealist in the literary sense. This one makes it happen.

It's not just word salad like a lot of articles that try to pull this sort of thing off; there's implication practically dripping from each entry. It's an exploration of this doctor's psyche that you're not even aware that you're on until you're deep into it. You also managed to make a Surrealistics article have a coherent narrative (as opposed to being a "wow, isn't this shit weird?" article) for what I think is the first time.

Enthusiastic +1. A fucking master-stroke of an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tanhony, you absolute mad man, take my upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It isn't often that I read an SCP and say aloud to myself "This shit is inspired." But that happened today. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My interpretation is a bit different.

+ My Theory
- Hide

-The writer was forced to do a job for the Chaos Insurgency
-The Chaos Insurgency (Represented by the Seeing Eye) could kill his daughter if he didn't give them enough intel (the black envelope).
- The Sun represents his feelings of joy or purpose in life, which is why the sun was extinguished when his daughter died.
- With his daughter dead, he came clean to the Foundation, and they sent him here, to have his cognition restructured to satiate SCP-5145, which is unrelated to his history. But his new brain won't fill 5145 forever, eventually he'll be torn apart, and some other mole will have to come take his place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Reply to Nietzsche
Finding More of the Rhytmn

Ahh…..
but I did mention the Chaos Insurgency.

Though I definitely did not notice the linguistic trick you pointed out (or is 'semantics' more accurate?)

Also…

just realized

Black envelope

envelope = mail

Blackmail…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoiler
– hide block

You're probably right about most of this but there's one thing you didn't mention. Seeing eye. See eye… CI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, from what I can tell….

My Theory
There's a Rhythm Beneath the Cacophony

- The writer of the scientist was forced to do a job for the Chaos Insurgency
- The Chaos Insurgency (represented by The Black Envelope) had his daughter
- The Seeing Eye was some device that they could use to kill his daughter should he not do this job
- This job involved stealing a deadly SCP (the Sun) that is related to the sun
- The writer failed, the result of this failure was worse than imagined: part of reality blown up. Daughter killed in process.
- Now he is writing a SCP for that part of reality that has been blown up by his actions, more specifically, the emotions of the action.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit what the fuck, why are you so good at this Tan, why why why? So good at making things I have no idea the true meaning of and yet I still enjoy reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Deeply unsettling, as always - we often describe someone or something as having a method to their madness. We rarely see how that method is developed, and it's just as unpleasant as one might think, it seems. I am acutely reminded of SCP-5101 though it's something of an inversion - salvador is much warmer in his mannerisms than Attendant JL, and the mental damage suffered here is more like being pulled apart than being crushed. In any case, excellent work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the kind of "what the fuck" I like to say; well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This leaves me with just the right number of questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Surrealistics" is right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so happy to see this finally up. Great job!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's what I personally call "weirdness".

+1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to DarnellJermaine, portalhugger, The Pighead, Boro610 and Hexick for their always valuable feedback!
